id: The Cattlestops-Abandoned Taproom Petone, 38 Fitzherbert St, Petone, Lower Hutt-Event Finder
name: The Cattlestops
venue: Abandoned Taproom Petone, 38 Fitzherbert St, Petone, Lower Hutt
long_description:
Celebrating twenty-one years of fine musicianship, this cowboy jazz country quartet are back on the road sharing their excellent songs and performance with and all.

With three CDs under their belt, of all original songs, and having performed on and contributed music for the NZ film ‘Secondhand Wedding’, featuring none other than the celebrated John Rowles, this band from the Kapiti Coast are performing the last in their latest mini cluster of great shows.

James Cameron on bass, Wayne Mason on keys, Evan Williams on drums and Andrew London on guitar will have you rocking and singing along to their stellar collection of songs.

RNZ NZ Live: The Cattlestops “Dance in the Rain”
www.youtube.com/watch?v=NdluyY8WHYs

To book: simply email bluegrass@xtra.co.nz
Include your name, phone contact and the number of tickets required
and you’ll be booked once you receive a reply to your email.

Come for dinner before the show!
Abandoned Taproom Petone have plenty of tasty treats, and grab a drink (or two) as well.

There is paid parking beside the venue in a monitored carpark at $2 per hour.
Alternatively free parking in any adjacent street.

id: Te Wā o Te Kōanga - Spring Festival-Wellington Botanic Garden, 101 Glenmore St, Kelburn, Wellington-Event Finder
name: Te Wā o Te Kōanga - Spring Festival
venue: Wellington Botanic Garden, 101 Glenmore St, Kelburn, Wellington
long_description:
Te Wā o te Kōanga | Spring Festival 2025
It’s time to shake off the winter blues and celebrate kōanga/spring at Wellington Gardens!

Join us throughout September for Te Wā o te Kōanga, a month-long festival bursting with flowers, fresh beginnings, and lots of ways to connect with nature and community.
Explore both Wellington Botanic Garden ki Paekākā and Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush with events for everyone — from self-drive floral tours and roaming performers, to interactive family fun, art exhibitions, and behind-the-scenes experiences.

Bring your friends, whānau, and neighbours to celebrate the season of new life in Aotearoa’s special native and cultivated gardens.
Stay tuned for event highlights and come discover the joy of kōanga!

Botanical Art Worldwide 2025
9am-4pm weekdays, 1-31 Aug
9am-4pm weekdays, 10am-4pm weekends, 1-30 Sept
Treehouse, Botanic Garden ki Paekākā

The Art of Ikebana Exhibition
9am-4pm, Sat 6 - Sun 7 Sept
Begonia House, Botanic Garden ki Paekākā

Spring Floral Display Accessible Self-Drive Tour
10am-3pm, Monday–Friday, 8-12 Sept, and 15-19 Sept.
Botanic Garden ki Paekākā

Rā Tūwhera ki Ōtari | Ōtari Open Day
10am–2pm, Sat 13 Sept
Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush

Te Āhua o te Haenga | Pollination in Motion
10am–2pm, Sat 20 Sept
Treehouse and Discovery Garden, Botanic Garden ki Paekākā

Kaiwhakaataata Māra | Garden Performers
10am–2pm, Sat 27 Sept
Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush & Botanic Garden ki Paekākā

Friends Garden walks
1:30pm-2:30pm, 6, 13, & 20 Sept
Botanic Garden ki Paekākā

Government House Garden Tour
10:30am, Friday 12 & 26 Sept
Government House gardens

For full event details, visit: wellingtongardens.nz/events/te-wa-o-te-koanga-spring-festival
description:
Te Wā o te Kōanga | Spring Festival 2025 It’s time to shake off the winter blues and celebrate kōanga/spring at Wellington Gardens!
Join us throughout September for Te Wā o te Kōanga, a month-long festival bursting with flowers, fresh beginnings, and lots of ways to connect with nature and community.
Explore both Wellington Botanic Garden ki Paekākā and Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush with events for everyone — from self-drive floral tours and roaming performers, to interactive family fun, art exhibitions, and behind-the-scenes experiences.
Bring your friends, whānau, and neighbours to celebrate the season of new life in Aotearoa’s special native and cultivated gardens.
Stay tuned for event highlights and come discover the joy of kōanga!

id: This Wasn't the Plan: A Musical Theatre Cabaret-Hannah Playhouse, 12 Cambridge Terrace, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
name: This Wasn't the Plan: A Musical Theatre Cabaret
venue: Hannah Playhouse, 12 Cambridge Terrace, Te Aro, Wellington
long_description:
Winner of Outstanding Solo Show and at the 2025 New Zealand Fringe Festival, and nominated for Best in Fringe. this show returns to The Hannah for one night only as part of the encore series during the Tahi Festival.
Please take your seats in this cabaret classroom as lecturer-performer Glenn Horsfall leads a hilarious, heartfelt lesson in what happens when life doesn’t go to plan. Featuring beautiful songs from a mix of beloved and lesser-known musical theatre favourites, and stories full of humour, heart, and reflection, this award-winning solo show invites you to embrace every version of yourself.
description:

id: Water Cast Pendants Taster - Spring-Workspace Studios Ltd, 65 Abel Smith St, Wellington-Event Finder
name: Water Cast Pendants Taster - Spring
venue: Workspace Studios Ltd, 65 Abel Smith St, Wellington
long_description:
This is a super quick 3-hour session, and is great fun to do with family or friends.

Water casting is a really hands-on low-tech casting method that results in surprising and fun little forms that can be turned into pendants with amazing textures and shapes.

You will be guided through melting silver and pouring it into water to create little charms.

Experiment with pouring at different heights to create different shapes.
Choose one or a few of your favourites to have drilled and turn into simple pendants or earrings, or new charms for your bracelets!

We will use hardened jump rings to attach your water-cast pieces to either colourful cord or you can choose sterling silver chains which you can purchase for extra.

The technique will be demonstrated with important health and safety practices, then students will be guided to do at least a couple of casts, and should expect to have at least one successful item to clean up and finish in the class.

A basic amount of metal allowance of a small amount of silver is included in your course costs, and if your projects go over that amount you can pay for the metal you use on the day via internet banking or cash.

This course is open to complete beginners and advanced makers. Extra materials such as chains and findings are available to purchase.

For health and safety reasons class participants must wear sturdy, closed toe shoes and have their hair tied back.
description:

A basic amount of metal allowance of a small amount of silver is included in your course costs, and if your projects go over that amount you can pay for the metal you use on the day via internet banking or cash.
This course is open to complete beginners and advanced makers.

venue: The Rogue and Vagabond, 18 Garrett St, Wellington
long_description:
IBrazza is Brazilian goodness on the dance floor at our favourite Wellington venue.

Join us at Rouge and Vagabundo to incinerate the darkness and boogie the night away.

A fusion of Brazilian Funk, famous classic tunes and original music that is sure to raise up the cold winter vibes.

Check out Abe the Babe in the kitchen, Blyn Blan Bliet and (Santo) Nicolau on wind, Frechie Fe on guitar, Pedro on bass, Fab directing traffic and Marcel keeping the pace up.

But let’s be honest, the beer is also stellar! We love Rogue!

Music from 9:30

FREE ENTRY

id: Ravel’s opera 'L'enfant et les sortilèges'-Hannah Playhouse, 12 Cambridge Terrace, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
name: Ravel’s opera 'L'enfant et les sortilèges'
venue: Hannah Playhouse, 12 Cambridge Terrace, Te Aro, Wellington
long_description:
New Zealand School of Music Classical Performance students present four performances Ravel's engagingly magical opera 'L'enfant et les sortilèges' at the Hannah Playhouse directed by Sara Brodie, conducted by Martin Riseley with the New Zealand School of Music orchestra.

Performed in French with subtitles, the show is suitable for all ages. The story involves a 'child' (L'enfant) whose awful behaviour brings an unexpected response: first the furniture rebels, the grandfather clock, the broken china cup, then the mathematics tutor and the Princess in the fairy tale book come to life, and eventually all the animals in the garden - squirrels, dragonfly, birds rise up - all of whom have good reason to 'teach the child a lesson'. It's a fabulous journey that we go on with the child who is confronted by all of these and indeed learns...

This is a project involving the whole of the Classical Performance programme: the voice students sing all the roles, each performing several roles, the NZSM orchestra will play for the performances, and the piano students are taking part as rehearsal pianists (répétiteurs) and playing in the orchestra.

Lighting design by Alex Fisher, set and costumes by Grace O’Brien, assistant director Theresa Hanaray, chorus master Kathryn Mosley, the show has been coordinated by NZSM Head of Classical Voice Jenny Wollerman in collaboration with the Toi Whakaari technical programme staff, graduates, and students
description:
New Zealand School of Music Classical Performance students present four performances Ravel's engagingly magical opera 'L'enfant et les sortilèges' at the Hannah Playhouse directed by Sara Brodie, conducted by Martin Riseley with the New Zealand School of Music orchestra.
Performed in French with subtitles, the show is suitable for all ages.
The story involves a 'child' (L'enfant) whose awful behaviour brings an unexpected response: first the furniture rebels, the grandfather clock, the broken china cup, then the mathematics tutor and the Princess in the fairy tale book come to life, and eventually all the animals in the garden - squirrels, dragonfly, birds rise up - all of whom have good reason to 'teach the child a lesson'.
It's a fabulous journey that we go on with the child who is confronted by all of these and indeed learns...
This is a project involving the whole of the Classical Performance programme: the voice students sing all the roles, each performing several roles, the NZSM orchestra will play for the performances, and the piano students are taking part as rehearsal pianists (répétiteurs) and playing in the orchestra.

id: Retirement Reinvented-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington-Event Finder
name: Retirement Reinvented
venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington
long_description:
Whether you’re already retired, or planning ahead, this practical workshop will help you to reflect, re-envision and re-vitalise your life and develop a plan for your future, on your own terms. (NZ citizen and residents free with ID, all others pay $109).

In your middle years, working, retired or somewhere in between? What’s next? Life is about much more than work or leisure, but most of us have given little thought to what “retirement” might mean.

It’s difficult to speculate what may happen in the future – but we can be proactive and take control.
This course offers insights into how to make the most of our time once we’re less focused on career and/or childrearing. It covers many of the issues people face when considering a move from current employment to a different lifestyle that includes more time to do the things we’ve always promised ourselves we’d do – “when we retire”.

After the workshop, you’ll be able to identify a number of common issues and potential ways to manage them when considering and planning your “rejuvenation”, rather than a retirement. We won’t cover financial planning, but will focus on some of the personal growth opportunities for living and making a conscious contribution to your community.

By the end of the day, you’ll have:
- Developed your own perspective on ageing and retirement in the 21st Century and identified what’s important to you
- Completed a personal life audit exercise and considered your strengths
- Explored possibilities in the major aspects of your life and identified potential challenges and strategies for overcoming them
- Developed a blueprint to maximise the best years of your life and developed a “future proofing” downsizing plan
- Considered options to include a sense of purpose and explored and implemented choices that provide you with that sense of purpose
Bring a pen and paper.

1 day, Sat 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM
Wellington High School
Presented by Angela Robertson
description:
Whether you’re already retired, or planning ahead, this practical workshop will help you to reflect, re-envision and re-vitalise your life and develop a plan for your future, on your own terms.
(NZ citizen and residents free with ID, all others pay $109).
What’s next?
After the workshop, you’ll be able to identify a number of common issues and potential ways to manage them when considering and planning your “rejuvenation”, rather than a retirement.
By the end of the day, you’ll have: - Developed your own perspective on ageing and retirement in the 21st Century and identified what’s important to you - Completed a personal life audit exercise and considered your strengths - Explored possibilities in the major aspects of your life and identified potential challenges and strategies for overcoming them - Developed a blueprint to maximise the best years of your life and developed a “future proofing” downsizing plan - Considered options to include a sense of purpose and explored and implemented choices that provide you with that sense of purpose Bring a pen and paper.

id: Negotiation Skills for Successful Outcomes-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington-Event Finder
name: Negotiation Skills for Successful Outcomes
venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington
long_description:
Everyone negotiates—at work, at home, or anywhere in between. Whether you're closing deals or resolving conflicts, learn how to get what you want and bring others on board with your vision. (NZ citizens and residents free with ID, others pay $100).

Description
Negotiation Skills for Successful Outcomes

What’s the Goal?
To arm you with the strategies, skills, and mindset to walk into any negotiation and leave with the outcome you desire.

What You'll Discover:

Crack the Code on People: Spot different types of negotiators and tailor your approach using the DISC methodology.
Gear Up for Success: Build a killer prep list with intel on your opponent and a clear inventory of your strengths.
Own the Room: Master the art of negotiation strategy, use space to your advantage, and know the unspoken rules.
Discover Your Edge: Tap into your natural negotiation style and make it work for you.
Learn from the Masters: Unlock game-changing tips and theories from the biggest “gurus” in the field.
Practice Makes Perfect: Get hands-on with exercises that turn theory into action.
Ready to negotiate like a pro? This workshop will show you how to make your case, win people over, and seal the deal.

You must be a NZ citizen or resident to register in this course for free and you must provide photo ID when you attend.

Note: Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

Sat 8 March 2025

1 day, Sat 9:30 AM - 3:30 PM
Wellington High School
Presented by Andrew Matushkin
$100.00 incl. GST
NZ Residents and Citizens $0.00 incl. GST

(NZ citizens and residents free with ID, others pay $100).

Own the Room: Master the art of negotiation strategy, use space to your advantage, and know the unspoken rules.

id: Paint and Wine Night in Waikanae Beach - Mothers Tulips-Hey Coastie, Wellington, 42 Tutere Street, Waikanae-Event Finder
name: Paint and Wine Night in Waikanae Beach - Mothers Tulips
venue: Hey Coastie, Wellington, 42 Tutere Street, Waikanae
long_description:
The concept is simple - take a brush in one hand, a drink in the other and let’s paint a masterpiece!

Join us for a creative night of drinking and painting in the heart of Waikanae! No previous painting experience necessary. Get your creative juices flowing and your brush strokes going!

Drinks: FIRST DRINK IS INCLUDED IN THE TICKET PRICE

Food: A delicious menu will be available to purchase throughout the event.

What is Paintvine?
It’s a fun social painting class where our artists will run you through step-by-step instructions on how to create your very own masterpiece in a fun and relaxed bar setting. Perfect for dates, catching up with mates or for meeting new friends!

We provide all the materials you need (high-quality canvas, acrylic paint, brushes). All you need to bring is your creative spirit (or find some at the bottom of a glass). At the end of the night you will leave with your own painting ready to hang up on your wall.

For tickets, future events, FAQs and more info head to the Paintvine website.
description:
The concept is simple - take a brush in one hand, a drink in the other and let’s paint a masterpiece!
No previous painting experience necessary.
What is Paintvine?
All you need to bring is your creative spirit (or find some at the bottom of a glass).
At the end of the night you will leave with your own painting ready to hang up on your wall.

id: Moon Jam Nite-Moon, 167 Riddiford Street, Wellington-Event Finder
name: Moon Jam Nite
venue: Moon, 167 Riddiford Street, Wellington
long_description:
Get your 15 minutes of fame at Newtown's famous Moon Jam Nite, every Tuesday from 7.30pm

The evening starts off as a regular open mic, and transforms into a full on jam towards the end.

Put your moniker into the sign up sheet, and own your time slot. You can call upon the many talented muso's available to help you play your slot.

A full stage with electric guitar, bass, keyboard, drums, mics, lights and full support from our sound tech.

Bring your own songs, poems, and friends (and make some new ones too)

Come along for dinner, drink specials & enjoy the show!

id: Girls Rock the Boys (Wellington)-Underworld Tavern, 13 Pirie Street, Wellington-Event Finder
name: Girls Rock the Boys (Wellington)
venue: Underworld Tavern, 13 Pirie Street, Wellington
long_description:
Girls Rock the Boys is bringing powerhouse vocals and legendary rock anthems to the stage. With Ashley Hale-Durham, Georgie Sullivan, Jude Madill, and Julie Lamb leading the charge, and a stellar band backing them up, it's bound to be a night of high-energy performances.

This from our last gig...

"Man did they ROCK! 4 very talented ladies and awesome band... the venue was rocking - check them out!"

An electrifying time warp! The 70s were a golden era for rock, with bands like Journey, Led Zeppelin, Whitesnake, and Deep Purple defining the sound of an entire generation. The idea of powerhouse female vocalists fronting a live band, belting out classics from these legends, is pure magic.

Early bird tickets are just $20, while door sales are $30—so it's definitely worth grabbing them early. This show is going to be an absolute blast, so securing those cheaper tickets ahead of time is a smart move.

4 very talented ladies and awesome band... the venue was rocking - check them out!"

id: Sustainable Fashion Market-Lower Hutt Event Centre , 30C Laings Road , Lower Hutt-Event Finder
name: Sustainable Fashion Market
venue: Lower Hutt Event Centre , 30C Laings Road , Lower Hutt
long_description:
NZ's biggest preloved clothing markets - Welcome to My Walk in Wardrobe!

Preloved, vintage, upcycled and handmade clothes, shoes, jewellery and more. All sizes, kids to adult.
Buy directly from closet clearouts to professional thrifters and talented makers.

Doors are open 10-2 pm.

To check out what the stallholders are bringing (including their sizes), go to our Facebook page, My Walk in Wardrobe, under 'events', then 'discussion.

Gold Coin Entry

Come at 10 - be prepared to line up
Come after 12 - for fewer crowds
Come after 1 - for more bargains

*Cash Is King* All stalls accept cash, some accept bank transfers and few will have Eftpos

For our FAQs or to be a stallholder:
www.mywalkinwardrobe.co.nz
description:
NZ's biggest preloved clothing markets - Welcome to My Walk in Wardrobe!
Preloved, vintage, upcycled and handmade clothes, shoes, jewellery and more.
All sizes, kids to adult.
Buy directly from closet clearouts to professional thrifters and talented makers.
Doors are open 10-2 pm.

id: Cooking Classes-Sheila's Indian Kitchen, Ashton Fitchett Dr, Wellington-Event Finder
name: Cooking Classes
venue: Sheila's Indian Kitchen, Ashton Fitchett Dr, Wellington
long_description:
Authentic Indian cooking classes in Wellington with panaromic views over Wellington Harbour.

In each class, you will learn 4-5 recipes like: potato stuff roti, roasted eggplant curry, wholemeal, roti, naan; shahi paneer; dhal, samosa; palak paneer; gourmet chickpeas; vegetable korma; gulab jamun; kheer; mango lassi; masala chai, and many more...

The classes start at 6 pm or 10:30 am on weekends and weekdays with a 2-hour session followed by a dinner or lunch afterwards. The ingredients, copies of recipes, organic karma drink will be provided with a full course meal in a banquet setting. Custom made classes available for gluten free, dairy free and any other food intolerance and dietary requirements.The ingredients, recipes, complimentary organic karma drinks will be provided with a full course meal.

There are 2 to 8 people in each class in purpose built cooking studio. Also a private cooking classes available.

The menu for the class can be:

1) Spicy potato stuffed paratha and roti, rajasthani tomato relish, aalu matter or spicy peas- potato curry, roasted cumin yogurt raita and fresh mint- coriander chutney.

2) Shahi Paneer (cottage cheese cubes with spices and rich tomato-cashew gravy), garlic coriander naan, cumin jeera basmati rice and sweet fragrant rice pudding with cardamom. Also I will teach how to make fresh paneer.

3) Roasted eggplant curry, plain whole wheat chapatti or roti, jeera aaloo or potato curry, Tuar dahl (split yellow lentil) and Basmati rice with homemade pickle.

4) Palak paneer (creamy spinach curry with cubes of paneer), jeera rice (basmati rice with fragrant spices, panjabi chhole or spicy chickpeas curry and mango lassi. Also fresh paneer making.

5) Khadi (chickpeas fritters in yogurt and chickpeas flour soupy gravy), Aloo-Gobhi (cauliflower and potato curry),  savoury wraps with herbs and spices, fresh pickle and basmati rice.

The total cost is $130 per person, this includes ingredient cost. (As an alternative we also sell cooking class gift vouchers.The food options are unlimited and the class provides fresh and best ingredients)
Please contact for booking: indiancooking@xtra.co.nz

So just bring your taste buds! and Enjoy!!
description:
The ingredients, copies of recipes, organic karma drink will be provided with a full course meal in a banquet setting.
There are 2 to 8 people in each class in purpose built cooking studio.
Also a private cooking classes available.
3) Roasted eggplant curry, plain whole wheat chapatti or roti, jeera aaloo or potato curry, Tuar dahl (split yellow lentil) and Basmati rice with homemade pickle.
4) Palak paneer (creamy spinach curry with cubes of paneer), jeera rice (basmati rice with fragrant spices, panjabi chhole or spicy chickpeas curry and mango lassi.

id: Yury Gezentsvey Plays Kreisler - 150th Anniversary Recital-St Hilda's Anglican Church, 311 The Parade, Island Bay, Wellington-Event Finder
name: Yury Gezentsvey Plays Kreisler - 150th Anniversary Recital
venue: St Hilda's Anglican Church, 311 The Parade, Island Bay, Wellington
long_description:
An hour recital of fifteen short pieces composed by one of the greatest violinists of all time, Fritz Kreisler.

Played by the renowned violinist Yury Gezentsvey and accompanied by Kirsten Simpson-Robertson on Piano.

An event not to be missed - book now!

This event aligns with the end of the South Coast Arts Trail. Come explore the beautiful South Coast and wrap up with a celebration of music at its finest, in the newly restored St Hilda's, winner of NZIA Small Project award at the 2024 New Zealand Architecture Awards.

Tickets $15 - $25
description:
An hour recital of fifteen short pieces composed by one of the greatest violinists of all time, Fritz Kreisler.
Played by the renowned violinist Yury Gezentsvey and accompanied by Kirsten Simpson-Robertson on Piano.
An event not to be missed - book now!
This event aligns with the end of the South Coast Arts Trail.
Come explore the beautiful South Coast and wrap up with a celebration of music at its finest, in the newly restored St Hilda's, winner of NZIA Small Project award at the 2024 New Zealand Architecture Awards.

id: Upper Hutt Repair Cafe-Whirinaki Whare Taonga, 836 Fergusson Drive, Upper Hutt-Event Finder
name: Upper Hutt Repair Cafe
venue: Whirinaki Whare Taonga, 836 Fergusson Drive, Upper Hutt
long_description:
Anyone can bring in their broken household items to be repaired for free or koha by volunteers, to help reduce landfill waste and poverty. Think the Repair Shop tv show.

Items we can help you repair: wooden, mechanical, electrical, bicycles, sewing, jewellery, frames, kids toys, porcelain dolls, clocks and everything in between.

id: Little Creators-Capital E, 4 Queens Wharf, Wellington-Event Finder
name: Little Creators
venue: Capital E, 4 Queens Wharf, Wellington
long_description:
Join us in PlayHQ for these 11am Thursday sessions.

Professor Inventus brings science, laughter and chaos to PlayHQ on Thursdays. Tamariki learn curious concepts about science, nature and the changing seasons.

Best suits ages 3-5, Science Jam is part of our E Tipu E Rea term-time programme.

Free, donations appreciated.

Nōku te Ao Capital E, 4 Queens Wharf. Pram parking and accessible bathrooms available.

id: Sunday Jazz - Jake Baxendale-The Rogue and Vagabond, 18 Garrett St, Wellington-Event Finder

The Rogue's regular Sunday afternoon jazz. Funky, jazzy, soulful jazz. Beer and jazz, on a Sunday perfect!

This week featuring local saxophonist Jake Baxendale

Music from 5pm, FREE ENTRY - Koha for the band encouraged.

id: Carmichael-The Rogue and Vagabond, 18 Garrett St, Wellington-Event Finder
name: Carmichael
venue: The Rogue and Vagabond, 18 Garrett St, Wellington
long_description:
Carmichael are back at it again, with their monthly instalment at the Rogue and Vagabond. Joined by very special guests & friends, come escape the final days of hōtoke with one hell of a dance party!
Carmichael is a six-piece jazz ensemble, based in Te Whanganui-a-Tara. Inspired by different global groove-driven collectives, such as Kokoroko, Ezra Collective, Nubiyan Twist & Fat Freddy’s Drop, their music blends elements of afrobeat, neo-soul and jazz fusion.

Featuring the beloved faces of the below Carmichaelians:

Lily Atu - alto sax & vocals
Elisa Bird - baritone sax
Frank Paton - trumpet
Darcy Monteath - bass
Petar Tavich - guitar
Joseph Alexander - drums
& special guests to be announced!

Music from 9:30pm, FREE ENTRY - Koha for the band encouraged
